threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You call it a 'shop' and not a 'corner shop', and your face lights up at the sight of 'local' artisan bread. They'll know you’re fresh off the bus.
Last night on the 35, a bloke serenaded us all with a ukulele. Smelled like takeaway and freedom. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a street artist painting a mural while a kebab shop’s smoke mingled with the scent of fresh bagels from the bakery next door.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a club buried inside an old sausage factory, complete with neon lights and a dance floor that used to be the meat locker.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they still know which taco truck’s salsa packs the best heat, even after a BART derailment.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the 38 bus line with an opera aria. All the riders paused, phones out, and for a moment, the chaos faded. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all bars close by 2 am, but many hidden locals stay open well past dawn, alive with energy and laughter.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their reluctance to step off the sidewalk when a pedestrian light blinks yellow—locals are always ready to dodge traffic.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newbie when they still think it's a good idea to take the tube during a strike. Just wait till the buses show up.
Heard a guy on the 59 bus trying to impress a girl with tales of his 'wild' nights at the Ritzy. Turns out, his idea of wild is a Tuesday quiz night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the BVG strike left my street eerily quiet, but I still heard the distant sounds of a spontaneous block party. That's Berlin.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the U-Bahn station at Kottbusser Tor always has a different street artist's work plastered under the escalator? It's like a mini gallery on your commute.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know that the BART maintenance delays are just another excuse not to ride the 14 or 49 during rush hour. We just laugh it off.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a pigeon hop on the 31-Balboa just as the doors closed. It looked around, then strutted down the aisle like it owned the place. Classic Muni.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about parks and plazas, but it’s the late-night chiringuitos and quirky shops that give it life after dark.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by the way they struggle to pronounce 'Gràcia' — it’s 'gra-thia', not 'gra-sia', folks.
